sourceText: This highlights Ashurbanipal's dual identity as both a scholar and a warrior, taking pride in his intellectual and physical prowess.
He was forcibly exiled to Spain in 1915 by the British for his resistance to colonial rule. He had to endure the pain and loneliness of exile, longing for his homeland.

Shawqi used his time in exile as a creative force, writing numerous masterpieces that celebrated the glory of Arab civilization and Egyptian identity. These poems further enhanced his reputation, and upon his return, he was welcomed as a national hero.
Stand for the teacher and grant him reverence, for the teacher was almost a messenger.
Emphasizes the sacred and vital role of educators in society, placing them on a near-prophetic level.
The small nations are the dust of history.
A stark reminder of the vulnerability of nations without power and unity, urging for national strength.
And for the homelands in the blood of every free man, is a hand that protects it and a tongue that speaks for it.
True patriotism involves both action (protection) and voice (advocacy) from its free citizens.
He struggled to prevent the decline of the Ottoman Empire in an era of rapid change, facing criticism and resistance from both conservative factions and radical reformers.
By compiling a systematic civil code, the Mecelle, and writing his extensive historical works, he sought to provide the empire with lasting order and identity through law and knowledge.
The science of history is the mother of all sciences.
This signifies that an understanding of the past is the essential foundation for grasping the present and planning for the future.
